MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-29-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
COURT CALL APPEARNCE:
JESSICA BARAJAS, ESQ OF BONONI LAW GROUP, 213-553-9200 FOR PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY AMANDA MALAVE

RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.
PLAINTIFF AMANDA MALAVE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS THERE WAS NO OPPOSITION FILED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, REPORTER: BELDON YEE CSR#: 2647. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (302/AJR)

Mtn Quashing Subpoenas Duces Tecum
Off Calendar Aug-02-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Raven Ward Sarnoff for plaintiff and moving party Amanda Malave
Tzaddi Smith for defendant and opposing party California State Automobile Assoication

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Amanda Malave's motion to quash deposition subpoenas is granted in part. The subpoenas are modified to only require production of any records concerning plaintiff that resulted in a court filing, complaint to any federal, state, or local governmental agency, and / or complaint pursuant to the employer's personnel policy relating to sexual harassment, failure to prevent sexual harassment, and wrongful termination in violation of the public policy against harassment. Any names, addresses, or other identifying information of third parties is ordered redacted. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
